factResults.renderedQuestion: Which inherited thrombophilia is the most common in the general population (~5% prevalence in Europeans), caused by a point mutation in Factor V that makes it resistant to cleavage by activated protein C (APC resistance)?
factResults.correctAnswer: Factor V Leiden: most common thrombophilia

distractors.answer: Crescentic GN: crescents in Bowman's capsule
